marketState: neutral, tilted bullish near-term on an extreme heat/demand spike but capped by a storage surplus and a bearish daily/weekly trend
summary: Front month NG ($2.92) sits right at model fair value ($2.917 now, FAIR status) across all horizons, so there is no fair-value mispricing edge to trade off directly. The standout signal is weather: 1-7D demand is +21% vs 5y and 8-16D demand is +31% vs 5y and above the historical max range, a genuine bullish catalyst for a cooling-season power-burn story. That is offset by a bearish fundamental backdrop - storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a +72 Bcf bearish surprise last week - and by managed-money positioned net short (though only at the 35th percentile, not an extreme). Seasonality is roughly a coin flip (44/56/38/38% odds of higher prices at 1/2/3/4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%) but is explicitly flagged Low reliability (59pp spread), so it should not be weighted heavily despite being the top-priority input. Technicals show a short intraday bullish pop (5m-1h RSI 55-65, positive MACD) sitting inside a still-bearish daily/weekly trend (RSI 35-37, negative MACD, 50<200 EMA), consistent with a weather-driven bounce inside a larger downtrend.

longTerm.sizing: 0.5-1R, approx 0.5-1% account risk (reduced for low conviction)
longTerm.rationale: Extreme above-max 8-16D cooling demand and a fair-value curve that drifts up from $2.92 now to $3.04 by +21D argue for a modest long, and price is running 7.4pp below its seasonal median which is a mild mean-reversion tailwind. This is tempered by a +184 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish weekly surprise and managed money still net short, plus Low-reliability seasonality (only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks), so conviction and size stay small.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus widens further or weather demand normalizes back toward the 5y average, or price closes below $2.70 confirming the daily/weekly downtrend resumes.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D demand is +21% vs 5y with a sharp +13.3 change in the last 24h, and intraday technicals (5m-1h) confirm bullish momentum with positive MACD and RSI 55-65, while the curve shows mild front-month backwardation vs Sep, consistent with prompt tightness. Front is trading at fair value with no discount to exploit, and 4h/1d/1w trend and momentum remain bearish, so this is treated as a tactical, tightly-stopped bounce trade rather than a high-conviction breakout.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily close back below $2.78 support or a fade in the heat forecast (1-7D demand reverting toward the 5y average) would invalidate the bounce thesis.

Front price trading at fair value across all horizons (no mispricing edge)
Storage +184 Bcf above 5y average with a +72 Bcf bearish surprise
Managed money net short (-60,295 contracts) but only 35th percentile vs 5y
Daily/weekly technicals bearish while intraday momentum has turned bullish
risks: Seasonality signal flagged Low reliability (59pp spread) - don't over-weight the 38% 3-4wk odds

Storage surplus and net-short positioning could reassert the daily/weekly downtrend if the heat catalyst fades
